Open Source Intel I’m glad Israel and Lebanon are sitting down. That’s an important first step. But I have some questions: Hezbollah isn’t some rogue outside militia Lebanon is fighting. It’s part of the Lebanese political system. For over 20 years, Lebanon has tried to disarm them and failed completely. Even the claim that Hezbollah was disarmed by Lebanon south of the Litani was a fake. Everyone knew it. So what exactly are we negotiating? Without real disarmament, this isn’t peace. It’s a pause. A few years for Hezbollah to rearm, regroup, and reset. Diplomacy with a government that has a powerful terror army it cannot control may end up masking Hezbollah rearming. Especially if Israel and the Lebanese army cannot coordinate, which is the likely case. And even if they try, it risks pushing Lebanon toward civil war. At the end of the day, real disarmament will only happen in one of two ways. Either Israel degrades Hezbollah to that point militarily, or Lebanon goes through internal collapse to remove it, aka civil war. Neither is clean. But pretending a signed agreement changes reality is how you end up right back here. I hope I’m wrong. Tell me how. tweet

Open Source Intel Sen. Bernie Sanders: "The Wall Street Journal reported recently that Jeff Bezos wants to raise $100 billion to automate factories in America and around the world. He wants to get rid of the 600,000 Amazon workers and replace them with robots." tweet

OSINTdefender The U.S. Department of the Treasury sent a letter to the United Arab Emirates (UAE), Hong Kong, China, and Oman advising them of banks within their jurisdiction that have allowed, potentiated, and benefited from Iranian illicit activity and asked them to take swift action against them and cease these activities. The letter, shared by Al-Monitor’s diplomatic correspondent Elizabeth Hagedorn, is the latest in a slew of Treasury actions relating to Iran. tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡ 🇮🇶 🇫🇷 🇮🇷 French Air Force Rafale fighter jets deployed in Iraq shot down at least two Iranian made Shahed-136 drones which were targeting Iranian-Kurdish opposition bases in the town of Koya. A higher resolution picture of one of the fighters shows its loadout. Three large external fuel tanks suggest that the aircraft was on an air patrol rather than scrambled from Erbil when it was tasked with the interception. A targeting pod can also be seen, used to improve detection and tracking of low flying and small targets such as drones. The aircrafts appear to be equipped with a pair of MICA air-to-air missiles. Maybe more were fired or are not visible. @wfwitness

🇱🇧 🇮🇱 𝗟𝗘𝗕𝗔𝗡𝗢𝗡–𝗜𝗦𝗥𝗔𝗘𝗟 𝗗𝗜𝗥𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗧𝗔𝗟𝗞𝗦: 𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗕𝗘𝗧𝗥𝗔𝗬𝗔𝗟 𝗢𝗙 𝗔 𝗡𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡 After years of struggle, resistance, blood, and martyrs, Lebanon now stands under a leadership imposed by the United States and Saudi Arabia, one that operates in alignment with Israel’s interests inside the country. The image emerging from the direct contacts and negotiations between Lebanon and “Israel”, conducted through ambassadors in Washington, does not reflect diplomacy, it reflects submission. A government and leadership that trade away rights, land, and dignity, offering concession after concession with nothing in return, only deeper losses carved into Lebanese sovereignty and paid for with the blood of its people. Such a government is not worthy of speaking on behalf of the majority of Lebanese, many of whom reject any form of negotiations or normalization with Israel. History will not be kind. President Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, in this view, will be remembered not as statesmen, but as figures who presided over the erosion of what generations fought and died to protect. These are not political compromises, they are seen as transactions made at the expense of a nation’s sacrifices. No matter how far these negotiations go, they do not decide the outcome. That is written elsewhere. On the ground, in the field, where the balance has always been shaped. There the decisive force remains the resistance, still standing, still fighting, still bearing the cost, refusing to surrender, refusing to abandon its people, and determined to prevent the enemy from ever imposing its will on Lebanese soil. Via: Ibrahim Majed on X 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇱🇾 🇮🇹 🇺🇸 The U.S.’s AFRICOM announced Libya, Italy, and the United States Exercise Flintlock 2026 in Libya, a multinational special forces operations exercise with forces from more than 30 nations across Côte d’Ivoire and Libya. The exercise will include coordinated air, ground, and maritime training aimed at improving combat readiness and operational cooperation among participating forces. Libya’s role in hosting part of the exercise is described as a milestone, with its forces facilitating training for over ten nations and signalling a commitment to regional security, while Italy has played a key role in planning and support. @wfwitness

The Trump administration has eased financial sanctions on Venezuela to help stabilize its struggling economy and address protests over low wages. The change allows major state banks, including the Banco Central de Venezuela, to use U.S. dollars, access oil revenues, and reconnect with the global financial system. Source: Axios

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 The commander of the 401st Armored Brigade’s 52nd Battalion, Lieutenant Colonel A., was seriously wounded today in fighting in southern Lebanon. The IDF said the circumstances of the incident are under review. The battalion commander was evacuated to hospital and his family has been notified. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇱🇧🇮🇱🇺🇸 Joint Israeli–Lebanese–U.S. statement Officials announced progress in Washington talks, outlining initial steps toward launching direct negotiations between Lebanon and Israel, with U.S. mediation: “Productive discussions were held on steps to launch…" ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Lebanon’s ambassador to Washington Nada Hamadeh Moawad described a preliminary meeting with the Israeli ambassador at the State Department as positive. She reaffirmed the urgent need for the full implementation of the November 2024 cessation of hostilities agreement, called for a ceasefire and the return of displaced people, and urged concrete steps to alleviate the severe humanitarian crisis in Lebanon. The date and location of the next meeting will be announced later. @wfwitness

🇱🇧 The Lebanese Amal Movement has rejected any form of direct negotiation with Israel, stating that the mechanism committee remains the practical and executive framework for halting hostilities and returning to full implementation of the October 2024 ceasefire terms, which Israel has not adhered to in any of its clauses. ➡️ Amal's executive committee reaffirms opposition to direct talks with the Israeli enemy ➡️ Mechanism committee remains the sole framework for ceasefire implementation ➡️ Israel has violated every clause of the October 2024 ceasefire agreement 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇨🇳 🇺🇸 Reuters: Eric Trump, son of U.S. President Trump, will accompany his father on a trip to China next month in a personal capacity, with his wife Lara. .The move has raised concerns about a possible conflict of interest, given that Trump's personal wealth and business dealings are managed by Eric and other members of his family. However, Eric has said he has no business interests in China The trip is aimed at maintaining stable relations between Beijing and Washington, with the two countries having agreed to a truce last year after a period marked by Trump's tariffs and China's restrictions on rare-earth exports. @wfwitness
🇱🇧 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 ⚡ The Lebanese Amal Movement rejects today's Israeli-Lebanese talks in Washington.

⚡ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Joint Israeli–Lebanese–U.S. statement Officials announced progress in Washington talks, outlining initial steps toward launching direct negotiations between Lebanon and Israel, with U.S. mediation: “Productive discussions were held on steps to launch direct negotiations. There is U.S. support for the Lebanese government’s plans to monopolize arms, and Washington hopes the talks will lead to a comprehensive peace agreement. Any Lebanese–Israeli ceasefire agreement will be under U.S. sponsorship and not through any separate track. The negotiations could lead to reconstruction and economic recovery for Lebanon. Israel supports the disarmament and dismantling of the infrastructure of armed groups in Lebanon, and it is committed to working with the Lebanese government on disarmament in a way that ensures the security of both countries. Lebanon affirms the principles of its territorial unity and its full sovereignty over its territory.” @wfwitness

🇰🇼 🇺🇸 ⚡️ Kuwaiti-American journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin has been locked up in Kuwait for 6 weeks, just for posting a video of a crashed U.S. fighter jet. He shared verified footage of the friendly-fire incident back in early March. Now he’s facing vague charges like “harming national security” and “spreading false info.”

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: Fwd from @ 📝 What Happened? 📝 On the Strait of Hormuz Blockade Despite announced blockade and control of the Strait of Hormuz by Donald Trump , the first results contradict expectations . Monitoring shows at least five vessels have passed through. 🔻 Whose Vessels Passed? ▪️ Confirmed: Rich Starry (China), Murlikishan (Madagascar), Peace Gulf (Panama) and Elpis (Comoros), and the bulk carrier Christianna (Liberia). Some previously faced US sanctions and worked with Iranian cargo. ▪️ Rich Starry, a Chinese vessel, departed UAE and entered the Gulf of Oman. ▪️ Peace Gulf is heading to UAE, Murlikishan to Iraq, not Iranian ports. ▪️ Elpis and Christianna departed Iranian ports after the blockade and passed through unhindered. 🖍 Estimates suggest up to 20 passages in 36 hours. Traffic has not been halted. 🚩 This occurs despite a dense US naval presence in the region, including the aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln . ❗️ Announcing a blockade is easier than enforcing it. Shipowners may take the risk if not calling at Iranian ports. 📌 The "blockade 2.0" seems more a show of presence than an actual closure - traffic continues, rules remain undefined. 📍 High resolution infographic 📍 English version #Iran #USA 🧨 @rybar_mena — on Middle Eastern chaos 💸

⚡️ 🇺🇦 🇮🇱 🇷🇺 Israeli Channel 12: Ukraine has asked Israel for clarification after a Russian “shadow fleet” ship allegedly carrying grain from "occupied Ukrainian territories" docked in Haifa port. The incident may constitute a significant violation of U.S. and international sanctions on Russia. Ukraine demands Israel stop the ship from leaving. A Ukrainian court issued an arrest warrant for the vessel on 8 April. Today, Ukraine’s prosecutor general met Israel’s ambassador in Kyiv and requested international legal assistance to detain the ship. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 The IDF said it carried out overnight strikes on multiple Hezbollah infrastructures in the village of Adshit in southern Lebanon. Around 130 rockets have been launched from the village since the start of operations, targeting Israeli forces and areas including Kiryat Shmona. The strikes reportedly targeted weapons depots, launchers, and command centres, and the IDF said Hezbollah fighters were involved in planning attacks against Israel were killed. @wfwitness

Norway will lift its ban on its $2.2 trillion sovereign wealth fund investing in Syrian government bonds. At the same time, Norway plans to add Iran to its exclusion list for such investments. Source: Reuters
